pattern: Maintain a ball-to-color map and a color-frequency map; when recoloring a ball decrement the old color's count (removing it if zero) and increment the new one.
hints: Each query recolors one ball. The color count can only increase or stay the same when a fresh ball is colored, and may decrease when a ball changes from a color used nowhere else.
Track ball -> current color, and color -> number of balls with that color. When you recolor a ball, update both maps.
After each update the answer is the number of entries in the color-frequency map with count > 0, which is just the size of the map if you remove entries when their count drops to zero.

edge_inputs.description: recoloring a ball to the same color
ball label at the limit boundary
many balls recolored to the same color then back
color disappears when last ball of that color is reassigned

stress.description: many queries mixing new colors and recolors
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    limit = 10**9
    queries = []
    used_balls = []
    for _ in range(n):
        if used_balls and rng.random() < 0.4:
            x = rng.choice(used_balls)
        else:
            x = rng.randint(0, limit)
            used_balls.append(x)
        y = rng.randint(1, 10**9)
        queries.append([x, y])
    return [limit, queries]
